description: Make screenshot_path resilient to screenshot-before-result races and invalid historical placeholder values.
steps: Reproduce the race where screenshot upload finishes before the first asin_data insert
Hydrate screenshot_path from screenshots on save_result and on result reads when the stored value is invalid
Verify with a regression test and a direct repro script
notes: Local data/scraper.db does not contain the user-reported ASINs, so verification used an isolated temp DB reproduction.

description: Enforce screenshot subprocess descendant cleanup so Chromium/browser children do not survive stop/restart and spike CPU.
steps: Launch screenshot subprocesses in their own process group
Kill the full process group on stop or unexpected exit
Make screenshot child request graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
Verify with syntax checks and a synthetic process-tree cleanup repro
notes: Real isolated batch validation showed screenshot pgid membership stayed flat (2 processes) and dropped to 0 on stop. Remaining screenshot issues are functional/state-machine problems tracked in review_list.json, not browser-process fan-out.

description: Isolate screenshot cache directories per worker/server so leaked or concurrent screenshot subprocesses cannot steal HTML files from another worker.
steps: Reproduce the disappearing-HTML race with diagnostic logs
Identify whether another process is consuming the shared screenshot cache
Namespace screenshot cache directories by server_url and worker_id
Verify an isolated batch reaches screenshot done=total before gate release
notes: The root cause of the disappearing HTML race was cross-process interference from orphan screenshot.py processes still watching the shared cache directory. Namespacing the cache by server+worker eliminated the theft in real validation.

description: Run staged screenshot load tests against the user-provided Excel file and confirm screenshot subprocess stability under hundreds of ASINs.
steps: Restart the local 8899 server on the current code
Run a first-stage 100-ASIN screenshot batch from the Excel source file
Increase to a larger screenshot batch from the same Excel file and monitor progress, CPU, and screenshot subprocess count
notes: Stage 1 (100 ASIN) and Stage 2 (400 ASIN) both completed with screenshots done=total on the live 8899 server. After restarting the worker onto the patched code, a dedicated 404 batch for B000F6RWW8 and B000LRX9XM also completed with screenshots done=2/2, confirming the missing-product placeholder path now closes cleanly. Residual risk is proxy-side 429s affecting throughput, not screenshot leakage or stuck pending rows.

description: Monitor the live 260,932-item batch with 4 workers, sample runtime behavior every minute, and generate a completion-time stability report.
steps: Identify the live batch and current worker topology
Expand to four workers and enable durable runtime log capture where safe
Run a minute-level monitor that records API progress, worker/process state, and log anomalies until completion
Generate a final report summarizing stability, throughput, and suspected bugs
notes: The monitor completed and wrote `.agent/evidence/f005-260k-runtime-report.md` after the target batch reached `pending=0` and `processing=0`. Final batch result was `257762 done`, `3170 failed`, `260932 total` (success rate `98.79%`). The run stayed stable enough to finish with 4 workers online, but the report captured sustained proxy `429`, timeout, blank-page, block/session-rotation, and degraded-page/parse-error patterns as the main operational risks.

description: Verify the new server-triggered worker soft-restart feature works end-to-end.
steps: Inspect the restart endpoint, sync payload, and worker restart handler
Start an isolated local server and worker for verification
Trigger `/api/workers/{worker_id}/restart` and confirm the worker rebuilds its session and stays online
Record the result and any defects with reproduction evidence
evidence: python3 -m py_compile server/app.py worker/engine.py run_server.py run_worker.py
notes: Isolated verification on `http://127.0.0.1:8898` passed: the server-side restart flag reached `restart-test-worker`, the worker rebuilt its session, stayed online, and completed a 200-ASIN batch with `done=200`, `failed=0`. Residual issue: the restart endpoint currently returns success even for nonexistent worker ids.

description: Analyze why some workers enter degraded-page / parse-error bursts immediately after `Session 初始化成功`, and propose concrete mitigations.
steps: Inspect the session initialization, product fetch, and degraded-page detection paths
Correlate the code with retained worker log evidence around session-init windows
Identify the most likely root causes and rank mitigations by expected impact
notes: The analysis concluded that the bursts are mainly caused by false-positive session readiness, no product-page canary, immediate 16-way cold-start traffic, and the fact that degraded parse failures do not currently invalidate the session. Recommended fix order: strict init verification + canary, cold-start probation, rotate on degraded-page clusters, then improve 200-level block telemetry/detection.
